1.o: All advantages, either as to outward circumstances, or spiritual privileges, come from the free love of God, who makes one to differ from another. All the evils sinners feel and fear, are the just recompence of their crimes, while all their hopes and comforts are from the unmerited mercy of the Lord. He chose his people that they might be holy. If we love him, it is because he has first loved us; yet we all are prone to undervalue the mercies of God, and to excuse our own offences.
1.m: All good things, whether they affect us externally or spiritually, come from God’s loving kindness. He treats each person differently based on His love. The bad things sinners experience are punishments for their wrongdoings, while their hopes and joys come from God’s undeserved kindness. God chose His people so they could be holy. We love Him only because He first loved us, but often we don’t appreciate God’s mercies enough and try to justify our mistakes.

1.o: We may each charge upon ourselves what is here charged upon the priests. Our relation to God, as our Father and Master, strongly obliges us to fear and honour him. But they were so scornful that they derided reproof. Sinners ruin themselves by trying to baffle their convictions. Those who live in careless neglect of holy ordinances, who attend on them without reverence, and go from them under no concern, in effect say, The table of the Lord is contemptible. They despised God's name in what they did. It is evident that these understood not the meaning of the sacrifices, as shadowing forth the unblemished Lamb of God; they grudged the expense, thinking all thrown away which did not turn to their profit. If we worship God ignorantly, and without understanding, we bring the blind for sacrifice; if we do it carelessly, if we are cold, dull, and dead in it, we bring the sick; if we rest in the bodily exercise, and do not make heart-work of it, we bring the lame; and if we suffer vain thoughts and distractions to lodge within us, we bring the torn. And is not this evil? Is it not a great affront to God, and a great wrong and injury to our own souls? In order to the acceptance of our actions with God, it is not enough to do that which, for the matter of it, is good; but we must do it from a right principle, in a right manner, and for a right end. Our constant mercies from God, make worse our slothfulness and niggardliness, in our returns of duty to God. A spiritual worship shall be established. Incense shall be offered to God's name, which signifies prayer and praise. And it shall be a pure offering. When the hour came, in which the true worshippers worshipped the Father in Spirit and in truth, then this incense was offered, even this pure offering. We may rely on God's mercy for pardon as to the past, but not for indulgence to sin in future. If there be a willing mind, it will be accepted, though defective; but if any be a deceiver, devoting his best to Satan and to his lusts, he is under a curse. Men now, though in a different way, profane the name of the Lord, pollute his table, and show contempt for his worship.
1.m:
We should take responsibility for the faults mentioned about the priests. As children of God, we are expected to respect and honor Him. However, the priests mocked those who tried to correct them. People hurt themselves by ignoring their conscience. Those who treat God’s commands casually, showing no respect during religious services, essentially say that His table is worthless. 

They showed disrespect toward God’s name through their actions. Clearly, they didn’t understand the deeper meaning behind the sacrifices, which pointed to Christ. They complained about the cost, seeing anything spent on religion as wasted unless it benefited them personally. 

If we worship God without truly knowing what we’re doing, we offer blind sacrifices. If we approach worship half-heartedly, we present offerings that are weak and lifeless. If we focus only on physical acts without putting our hearts into it, we bring lame sacrifices. And if we let distracting thoughts interfere, we bring torn offerings. Isn't this wrong? Doesn’t it insult God and harm our own souls?

For our worship to be acceptable to God, we need more than just doing good things. We must have the right motives, follow proper methods, and aim for genuine devotion. Our daily blessings from God should motivate us to give back faithfully. True worship will eventually come, involving sincere prayers and praises. This will be an honest offering.

We can trust God’s mercy to forgive past mistakes, but He won’t allow ongoing sins. If someone tries sincerely, even if imperfectly, God accepts it. But if anyone pretends while secretly serving their desires or Satan, they face a curse. Today, people still dishonor God’s name, misuse His table, and show disdain for His worship, though in different ways.

2.o: What is here said of the covenant of priesthood, is true of the covenant of grace made with all believers, as spiritual priests. It is a covenant of life and peace; it assures all believers of all happiness, both in this world and in that to come. It is an honour to God's servants to be employed as his messengers. The priest's lips should not keep knowledge from his people, but keep it for them. The people are all concerned to know the will of the Lord. We must not only consult the written word, but desire instruction and advice from God's messengers, in the affairs of our souls. Ministers must exert themselves to the utmost for the conversion of sinners; and even among those called Israelites, there are many to be turned from iniquity. Those ministers, and those only, are likely to turn men from sin, who preach sound doctrine, and live holy lives according to the Scripture. Many departed from this way; thus they misled the people. Such as walk with God in peace and righteousness, and turn others from sin, honour God; he will honour them, while those who despise him shall be lightly esteemed.
2.m:
Here we talk about the agreement between God and believers, which is like the agreement between God and priests. This agreement promises life and peace to everyone who believes. 

It’s an honor for God’s workers to serve as His messengers. Priests shouldn’t hide what they know from their people but share it with them. People need to learn God’s will. 

We should look at the Bible and ask God’s messengers for help with matters of our soul. Ministers must do everything they can to bring sinners back to God. Even among those called Israelites, there are many who need to be turned away from sin. Only ministers who teach correctly and live good lives based on the Bible can truly lead people away from sin.

Many people left this path, which led others astray. Those who walk with God in peace and righteousness and help others leave sin behind honor Him. God honors such people, while those who ignore Him are seen as unimportant.

2.o: Corrupt practices are the fruit of corrupt principles; and he who is false to his God, will not be true to his fellow mortals. In contempt of the marriage covenant, which God instituted, the Jews put away the wives they had of their own nation, probably to make room for strange wives. They made their lives bitter to them; yet, in the sight of others, they pretend to be tender of them. Consider she is thy wife; thy own; the nearest relation thou hast in the world. The wife is to be looked on, not as a servant, but as a companion to the husband. There is an oath of God between them, which is not to be trifled with. Man and wife should continue to their lives' end, in holy love and peace. Did not God make one, one Eve for one Adam? Yet God could have made another Eve. Wherefore did he make but one woman for one man? It was that the children might be made a seed to serve him. Husbands and wives must live in the fear of God, that their seed may be a godly seed. The God of Israel saith that he hateth putting away. Those who would be kept from sin, must take heed to their spirits, for there all sin begins. Men will find that their wrong conduct in their families springs from selfishness, which disregards the welfare and happiness of others, when opposed to their own passions and fancies. It is wearisome to God to hear people justify themselves in wicked practices. Those who think God can be a friend to sin, affront him, and deceive themselves. The scoffers said, Where is the God of judgement? but the day of the Lord will come.
2.m: Bad actions come from bad beliefs. Someone who isn’t faithful to God won’t be honest with other people either. Some Jewish men ignored God’s rules about marriage by divorcing their wives. They did this to marry foreign women instead. They were mean to their wives but pretended to care about them in front of others. Remember, she is your wife—your partner and closest family member. Treat her like a partner, not a servant. You both have a promise from God that you need to keep. Husband and wife should stay together until death. Didn't God create just one Eve for Adam? He could have made more wives if he wanted to. Why only one? So their children could honor God. Families should respect God so their kids grow up to be good too. God hates divorce. People avoid sinning by watching their thoughts because that’s where sin starts. Selfishness leads to mistreating family members. When people try to excuse their wrong behavior, it makes God sad. Thinking God approves of sin is insulting him and fooling yourself. Skeptics ask, “Where is God?” But God’s judgment day will come.

3.o: The first words of this chapter seem an answer to the scoffers of those days. Here is a prophecy of the appearing of John the Baptist. He is Christ's harbinger. He shall prepare the way before him, by calling men to repentance. The Messiah had been long called, “He that should come,” and now shortly he will come. He is the Messenger of the covenant. Those who seek Jesus, shall find pleasure in him, often when not looked for. The Lord Jesus, prepares the sinner's heart to be his temple, by the ministry of his word and the convictions of his Spirit, and he enters it as the Messenger of peace and consolation. No hypocrite or formalist can endure his doctrine, or stand before his tribunal. Christ came to distinguish men, to separate between the precious and the vile. He shall sit as a Refiner. Christ, by his gospel, shall purify and reform his church, and by his Spirit working with it, shall regenerate and cleanse souls. He will take away the dross found in them. He will separate their corruptions, which render their faculties worthless and useless. The believer needs not fear the fiery trial of afflictions and temptations, by which the Saviour refines his gold. He will take care it is not more intense or longer than is needful for his good; and this trial will end far otherwise than that of the wicked. Christ will, by interceding for them, make them accepted. Where no fear of God is, no good is to be expected. Evil pursues sinners. God is unchangeable. And though the sentence against evil works be not executed speedily, yet it will be executed; the Lord is as much an enemy to sin as ever. We may all apply this to ourselves. Because we have to do with a God that changes not, therefore it is that we are not consumed; because his compassions fail not.
3.m:
This chapter starts by answering people who were making fun of what was coming. It talks about John the Baptist showing up before Jesus. John would get people ready for Jesus by telling them to turn from their sins. For a long time, people talked about "the one who would come." Now, soon, he really will arrive. 

Jesus is the Messenger of peace and comfort. People who look for Jesus will enjoy finding him, even when they don't expect it. Jesus uses his Word and Spirit to get people ready to receive him. Then he comes as the Messenger of peace.

Hypocrites and fake believers cannot handle Jesus' teachings or face his judgment. Jesus separates people, choosing the good over the bad. He acts like a Refiner, cleaning out impurities. Through his gospel and the work of his Spirit, Jesus reforms his church and cleanses hearts.

Jesus removes the bad parts inside us, just like removing dross from metal. Believers shouldn’t worry about trials and tests, because Jesus watches over them. These trials won’t last too long or hurt too much. They help believers grow stronger.

God doesn’t change. Even if punishment for wrongdoings isn’t immediate, it will happen eventually. God hates sin just as much today as always. We can trust God’s promises because he never changes. His love and mercy never run out.

3.o: The men of that generation turned away from God, they had not kept his ordinances. God gives them a gracious call. But they said, Wherein shall we return? God notices what returns our hearts make to the calls of his word. It shows great perverseness in sin, when men make afflictions excuses for sin, which are sent to part between them and their sins. Here is an earnest exhortation to reform. God must be served in the first place; and the interest of our souls ought to be preferred before that of our bodies. Let them trust God to provide for their comfort. God has blessings ready for us, but through the weakness of our faith and the narrowness of our desires, we have not room to receive them. He who makes trial will find nothing is lost by honouring the Lord with his substance.
3.m:
The people of that time stopped following God and did not keep his rules. God called them back kindly. They asked, “What should we do differently?” 

God sees how we respond to his invitations. Sinners often use troubles as reasons to continue sinning, even though those troubles were meant to stop them from sinning. This shows how stubbornly sinful people can be.

There’s a strong plea here to change their ways. We must put God first and care more about our souls than our bodies. Trust God to take care of you. 

He already has good things ready for us, but because of weak faith and small desires, we don’t have enough space to accept them all. When someone tests honoring God with their resources, they discover there’s no loss in doing so.

3.o: Among the Jews at this time, some plainly discovered themselves to be children of the wicked one. The yoke of Christ is easy. But those who work wickedness, tempt God by presumptuous sins. Judge of things as they will appear when the doom of these proud sinners comes to be executed. Those that feared the Lord, spake kindly, for preserving and promoting mutual love, when sin thus abounded. They spake one to another, in the language of those that fear the Lord, and think on his name. As evil communications corrupt good minds and manners, so good communications confirm them. A book of remembrance was written before God. He will take care that his children perish not with those that believe not. They shall be vessels of mercy and honour, when the rest are made vessels of wrath and dishonour. The saints are God's jewels; they are dear to him. He will preserve them as his jewels, when the earth is burned up like dross. Those who now own God for theirs, he will then own for his. It is our duty to serve God with the disposition of children; and he will not have his children trained up in idleness; they must do him service from a principle of love. Even God's children stand in need of sparing mercy. All are righteous or wicked, such as serve God, or such as serve him not: all are going to heaven or to hell. We are often deceived in our opinions concerning both the one and the other; but at the bar of Christ, every man's character will be known. As to ourselves, we have need to think among which we shall have our lot; and, as to others, we must judge nothing before the time. But in the end all the world will confess that those alone were wise and happy, who served the Lord and trusted in Him.
3.m: At this time, some people showed they were children of the devil. The teachings of Jesus are easy to follow. But those who do bad things test God with their sins. Think about how proud sinners will look when they face judgment. People who respected God spoke kindly to each other, showing love. They talked to one another using words that show respect for God. Bad company can ruin good habits, while good company helps keep them strong. There’s a record in heaven of God’s faithful followers. He’ll protect them from those who don’t believe. His followers will be honored, while others will be punished. God sees his believers as precious treasures. He will save them like valuable jewels when the earth burns. Now, if we belong to God, he will admit us as his children later. Our job is to serve God like loving children. God expects his children to work hard, not be lazy. Even God’s children sometimes need mercy. Everyone either serves God or doesn’t. Some go to heaven, others to hell. Often, we’re wrong about who belongs where. At the final judgment, everyone’s true nature will be revealed. We should wonder where we fit in. And we shouldn’t judge others until the end. In the end, only those who loved and obeyed God will truly be wise and happy.

4.o: Here is a reference to the first and to the second coming of Christ: God has fixed the day of both. Those who do wickedly, who do not fear God's anger, shall feel it. It is certainly to be applied to the day of judgment, when Christ shall be revealed in flaming fire; to execute judgment on the proud, and all that do wickedly. In both, Christ is a rejoicing Light to those who serve him faithfully. By the Sun of Righteousness we understand Jesus Christ. Through him believers are justified and sanctified, and so are brought to see light. His influences render the sinner holy, joyful, and fruitful. It is applicable to the graces and comforts of the Holy Spirit, brought into the souls of men. Christ gave the Spirit to those who are his, to shine in their hearts, and to be a Comforter to them, a Sun and a Shield. That day which to the wicked will burn as an oven, will to the righteous be bright as the morning; it is what they wait for, more than those that wait for the morning. Christ came as the Sun, to bring, not only light to a dark world, but health to a distempered world. Souls shall increase in knowledge and spiritual strength. Their growth is as that of calves of the stall, not as the flower of the field, which is slender and weak, and soon withers. The saints' triumphs are all owing to God's victories; it is not they that do this, but God who does it for them. Behold another day is coming, far more dreadful to all that work wickedness than any which is gone before. How great then the happiness of the believer, when he goes from the darkness and misery of this world, to rejoice in the Lord for evermore!
4.m:
This talks about the first and second coming of Christ. God set the date for both times. People who do bad things and don’t fear God’s punishment will face it. This applies to the final judgment when Christ will come in flames to punish the proud and those who act wrongly. 

In both cases, Christ brings joy to those who follow him faithfully. We call Jesus the Sun of Righteousness because through him, believers are made right and pure, seeing the light. His influence makes sinners holy, happy, and productive. 

It also fits the gifts and comfort of the Holy Spirit given to believers. Christ sends the Spirit to shine in their hearts and be a Comforter, like a sun and shield. For the wicked, that day will be like burning in an oven, but for the righteous, it will be as bright as morning—something they eagerly await. 

Christ came like the Sun to give light and healing to a troubled world. Faithful people grow stronger spiritually, just like calves growing in a stable, not like flowers that quickly wilt. The saints’ victories come from God’s power, not their own efforts. 

Look forward to another day that will be even worse for those who do evil. But for believers, leaving this dark and miserable world means endless joy with the Lord.

4.o: Here is a solemn conclusion, not only of this prophecy, but of the Old Testament. Conscience bids us remember the law. Though we have not prophets, yet, as long as we have Bibles, we may keep up our communion with God. Let others boast in their proud reasoning, and call it enlightening, but let us keep near to that sacred word, through which this Sun of Righteousness shines upon the souls of his people. They must keep up a believing expectation of the gospel of Christ, and must look for the beginning of it. John the Baptist preached repentance and reformation, as Elijah had done. The turning of souls to God and their duty, is the best preparation of them for the great and dreadful day of the Lord. John shall preach a doctrine that shall reach men's hearts, and work a change in them. Thus he shall prepare the way for the kingdom of heaven. The Jewish nation, by wickedness, laid themselves open to the curse. God was ready to bring ruin upon them; but he will once more try whether they will repent and return; therefore he sent John the Baptist to preach repentance to them. Let the believer wait with patience for his release, and cheerfully expect the great day, when Christ shall come the second time to complete our salvation. But those must expect to be smitten with a sword, with a curse, who turn not to Him that smites them with a rod. None can expect to escape the curse of God's broken law, nor to enjoy the happiness of his chosen and redeemed people, unless their hearts are turned from sin and the world, to Christ and holiness. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with us all. Amen.
4.m:
This is a serious ending not just for this prophecy, but for the whole Old Testament. Our conscience tells us to remember the law. Even if we don't have prophets anymore, we can still stay close to God by having Bibles. Others might brag about their smart thinking, calling it enlightenment, but we should stick to the holy words. This is where the light of the Sun of Righteousness shines on the souls of God’s people.

We need to believe that the good news about Christ is coming soon. We should look forward to its start. John the Baptist taught people to repent and change their ways, just like Elijah did before him. Turning back to God and doing what’s right prepares people for the big and scary day when God judges everyone.

The Jewish people were being very bad, so they deserved punishment. God wanted to punish them, but first He tried to see if they would change. So He sent John the Baptist to tell them to repent. Believers should wait patiently for their freedom and joyfully look forward to the day when Christ comes again to finish saving us.

But those who don’t turn to God after He warns them will face His anger. People won’t avoid God’s punishment or enjoy His blessings unless they stop sinning and turn to Christ instead. May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of us. Amen.
